verdict_reasoning: CTAG1A (NY-ESO-1) is a cancer/testis antigen with no documented transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or membrane-anchoring mechanism. It is a cytoplasmic/intracellular antigen whose primary biology is MHC-mediated antigen presentation. Although it is a target of TCR-T and other cancer immunotherapies, these work via MHC-peptide recognition, not via surface accessibility of the protein body itself. The protein body does not reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane.
